generatedSentence: Officer Kael, a lovable rider of mechanical unicorns, affirmed his resolution against the everyday mayhem, enjoying a merry poolside scene with his family. His uniform, though not revealing, had a coppery, upswept design. He watched his goldfishes in a pond near the gazebo, an obvious amenity in the local scenery. A nubile falcon, his 'mommy'-figure, often offered him a premium freebie. He felt a pinch of growth, a new venture far from kindergarten rules, yet still an ordinate part of nature. This tranquil daydream was shattered by a massive thump, a hydraulic slowdown. A berserk vervet monkey, escaping a closet, had sabotaged the airway's teleportation system. This nebulous letdown was no joke; it was an actual killjoy factorial problem, demanding maximum effort to unscramble. A variable vampire knight, known for racketeering and wars, observed from a diagonal vesture in the shadows, his teeth gleaming. He seemed obsessed with the chaos, lampooning the situation against Kael's will. Kael, with newfound resolution, knew he had to act. He saw the villain, a hired ruler with a rubbery vesture, orchestrating the calamity from a nearby newsstand. He'd promised his family a vacation, a definite payout, not this unfolding horror. Kael's falcon, usually a calm officer, went berserk, causing an updraft as she attacked the vampire's militia, who were now wielding razors. A gunshot echoed through the lobby, a clear signal of the climax. Kael felt his knuckle crack, preparing for the inevitable fight. He confronted the warden, who, rather than fixing the slowdown, was busy with voicemails and estimations for deforestation. Kael, against the backdrop of jetsam, rewrote the entire operational keyword, a true testament to his effort. His amigos, donning floral dressing gowns made of rayon, helped unwind the remaining chaos. The kittening unicorn foal, now safe, symbolized hope. Kael made a solemn pledge, for his family and nation, a new beginning after the mayhem, securing his well-deserved paycheck.
panelSentences: Officer Kael, a lovable rider of mechanical unicorns, affirmed his resolution against the everyday mayhem, enjoying a merry poolside scene with his family. His uniform, though not revealing, had a coppery, upswept design. He watched his goldfishes in a pond near the gazebo, an obvious amenity in the local scenery. A nubile falcon, his 'mommy'-figure, often offered him a premium freebie. He felt a pinch of growth, a new venture far from kindergarten rules, yet still an ordinate part of nature.

This tranquil daydream was shattered by a massive thump, a hydraulic slowdown. A berserk vervet monkey, escaping a closet, had sabotaged the airway's teleportation system. This nebulous letdown was no joke; it was an actual killjoy factorial problem, demanding maximum effort to unscramble. A variable vampire knight, known for racketeering and wars, observed from a diagonal vesture in the shadows, his teeth gleaming. He seemed obsessed with the chaos, lampooning the situation against Kael's will.

Kael, with newfound resolution, knew he had to act. He saw the villain, a hired ruler with a rubbery vesture, orchestrating the calamity from a nearby newsstand. He'd promised his family a vacation, a definite payout, not this unfolding horror. Kael's falcon, usually a calm officer, went berserk, causing an updraft as she attacked the vampire's militia, who were now wielding razors. A gunshot echoed through the lobby, a clear signal of the climax. Kael felt his knuckle crack, preparing for the inevitable fight.

He confronted the warden, who, rather than fixing the slowdown, was busy with voicemails and estimations for deforestation. Kael, against the backdrop of jetsam, rewrote the entire operational keyword, a true testament to his effort. His amigos, donning floral dressing gowns made of rayon, helped unwind the remaining chaos. The kittening unicorn foal, now safe, symbolized hope. Kael made a solemn pledge, for his family and nation, a new beginning after the mayhem, securing his well-deserved paycheck.
rawPanelSentences: Officer Kael, a lovable rider of mechanical unicorns, affirmed his resolution against the everyday mayhem, enjoying a merry poolside scene with his family. His uniform, though not revealing, had a coppery, upswept design. He watched his goldfishes in a pond near the gazebo, an obvious amenity in the local scenery. A nubile falcon, his 'mommy'-figure, often offered him a premium freebie. He felt a pinch of growth, a new venture far from kindergarten rules, yet still an ordinate part of nature.

This tranquil daydream was shattered by a massive thump, a hydraulic slowdown. A berserk vervet monkey, escaping a closet, had sabotaged the airway's teleportation system. This nebulous letdown was no joke; it was an actual killjoy factorial problem, demanding maximum effort to unscramble. A variable vampire knight, known for racketeering and wars, observed from a diagonal vesture in the shadows, his teeth gleaming. He seemed obsessed with the chaos, lampooning the situation against Kael's will.

Kael, with newfound resolution, knew he had to act. He saw the villain, a hired ruler with a rubbery vesture, orchestrating the calamity from a nearby newsstand. He'd promised his family a vacation, a definite payout, not this unfolding horror. Kael's falcon, usually a calm officer, went berserk, causing an updraft as she attacked the vampire's militia, who were now wielding razors. A gunshot echoed through the lobby, a clear signal of the climax. Kael felt his knuckle crack, preparing for the inevitable fight.

He confronted the warden, who, rather than fixing the slowdown, was busy with voicemails and estimations for deforestation. Kael, against the backdrop of jetsam, rewrote the entire operational keyword, a true testament to his effort. His amigos, donning floral dressing gowns made of rayon, helped unwind the remaining chaos. The kittening unicorn foal, now safe, symbolized hope. Kael made a solemn pledge, for his family and nation, a new beginning after the mayhem, securing his well-deserved paycheck.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined male officer with a coppery, upswept uniform and glowing gauntlets. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Officer Kael, in profile, standing by a futuristic poolside gazebo, a small robotic falcon perched on his shoulder, observing his family playing. (Mid-shot, slightly high angle). TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael squinting, looking up at a chaotic, damaged futuristic airway tube with a berserk vervet monkey clinging to it, while a shadowy vampire knight watches from a diagonal alleyway. (Wide shot, dynamic low angle). BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael in a martial stance, gauntlets glowing, protecting his falcon from a charging militia member with a razor, a gunshot muzzle flash in the background. (Close-up, dramatic high-angle). BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, victorious but weary, helping a small, glowing unicorn foal rise from a pile of jetsam, while his friends in floral rayon robes look on in the distance. (Medium shot, heroic eye-level).

definitions.definition: A strong will, determination.
The state of being resolute.
definitions.example: His stalwart resolution is perhaps admirable, perhaps foolish.

definitions.definition: A statement of intent, a vow
definitions.example: By February, most New Year's resolutions are forgotten.   My resolution is to cut back on the fast food this year.

definitions.definition: The act of discerning detail.
(computing, photography) The degree of fineness with which an image can be recorded or produced, often expressed as the number of pixels per unit of length (typically an inch).
definitions.example: Printing at higher resolution will cause a reduction in performance.

definitions.definition: The number of pixels in an image being stored or displayed.
definitions.example: This monitor's maximum resolution is 1600 × 1200.

definitions.definition: The process of determining the meaning of a symbol or address; lookup.
definitions.example: name resolution

definitions.definition: The act or process of solving; solution.
definitions.example: the resolution of an equation

definitions.definition: A formal statement adopted by an assembly, or during any other formal meeting.
definitions.example: The resolution was passed by a two-thirds majority.

definitions.definition: The separation of the constituent parts (of a spectrum etc).
The degree of fineness of such a separation.
Progression from dissonance to consonance; a chord to which such progression is made.
The moment in which the conflict ends and the outcome of the action is clear.
In a pathological process, the phase during which pathogens and damaged tissues are removed by macrophages.
One who rides, often on a horse or a motorcycle.
A provision annexed to a bill under the consideration of a legislature, having little connection with the subject matter of the bill.
(by extension) Something extra or burdensome that is imposed.
An amendment or addition to an entertainer's performance contract, often covering a performer's equipment or food, drinks, and general comfort requirements.
An additional benefit attached to an insurance contract.
A small, sliding piece of aluminium on a chemical balance, used to determine small weights.
An agent who goes out with samples of goods to obtain orders; a commercial traveller.
One who breaks in or manages a horse.
The first Lenormand card, also known as either the horseman or the cavalier.
A problem of extra difficulty added to another on an examination paper.
An old Dutch gold coin with the figure of a man on horseback stamped upon it.
Rock material in a vein of ore, dividing it.
An interior rib occasionally fixed in a ship's hold, reaching from the keelson to the beams of the lower deck, to strengthen the frame.
The second tier of casks in a vessel's hold.
A small forked weight which straddles the beam of a balance, along which it can be moved in the manner of the weight on a steelyard.
A robber.
A piece, such as the rook or bishop, which moves any distance in one direction, as long as no other piece is in the way.
A mythical beast resembling a horse or deer with a single, straight, spiraled horn projecting from its forehead.
In various Bible translations, used to render the Latin unicornis or rhinoceros (representing Hebrew רְאֵם): a reem or wild ox.
Any large beetle having a horn-like prominence on the head or prothorax, especially the Hercules beetle, Dynastes tityus.
A caterpillar, Schizura unicornis, with a large thorn-like spine on the back near its head.
The kamichi, or unicorn bird.
A howitzer.
Someone or something that is rare and hard to find.
Being many (especially pastel) colours; multicoloured.
definitions.example: unicorn smoothies

definitions.definition: A 15th-century Scottish gold coin worth 18 shillings, bearing the image of a unicorn.
(sexual slang) To participate in a sexual threesome as a bisexual addition to an established heterosexual couple.
To exceed a valuation of one billion U.S. dollars, while solely backed by venture capitalists.
One who has a position of authority in a hierarchical organization, especially in military, police or government organizations.
A respectful term of address for an officer, especially a police officer.
One who holds a public office.
An agent or servant imparted with the ability, to some degree, to act on initiative.
A commissioned officer.
To supply with officers.
To command like an officer.
A group of people who are closely related to one another (by blood, marriage or adoption); kin; for example, a set of parents and their children; an immediate family.
definitions.example: Our family lives in town.

definitions.definition: An extended family; a group of people who are related to one another by blood or marriage.
definitions.example: 1915, William T. Groves, A History and Genealogy of the Groves Family in America

definitions.definition: A (close-knit) group of people related by blood, friendship, marriage, law, or custom, especially if they live or work together.
definitions.example: Our company is one big happy family.

definitions.definition: Lineage, especially an honorable one
A rank in the classification of organisms, below order and above genus; a taxon at that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the family Magnoliaceae.

definitions.definition: Any group or aggregation of things classed together as kindred or related from possessing in common characteristics which distinguish them from other things of the same order.
definitions.example: Doliracetam is a drug from the racetam family.

definitions.definition: A group of instruments having the same basic method of tone production.
definitions.example: the brass family;  the violin family

definitions.definition: A group of languages believed to have descended from the same ancestral language.
definitions.example: the Indo-European language family;  the Afroasiatic language family

definitions.definition: Used attributively.
definitions.example: For Apocynaceae, this type of flower is a family characteristic.

definitions.definition: Suitable for children and adults.
definitions.example: It's not good for a date, it's a family restaurant.

definitions.definition: Conservative, traditional.
definitions.example: The cultural struggle is for the survival of family values against all manner of atheistic amorality.

definitions.definition: Homosexual.
definitions.example: I knew he was family when I first met him.

definitions.definition: A person belonging to a particular family; a close relative or relation.
The area beside a pool.
By the side of a pool.
definitions.example: She was sunbathing on the poolside deckchair.

definitions.definition: Beside a pool.
To uncover; to show and display that which was hidden.
To communicate that which could not be known or discovered without divine or supernatural instruction.
Something revealed; a revelation.
Informative.
definitions.example: a revealing analysis

definitions.definition: Of clothing: allowing more than is usual to be seen.
definitions.example: Her shirt is rather revealing.

definitions.definition: A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
Phonetic equivalent for the letter U in the ICAO spelling alphabet, informally known as the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A uniformed police officer (as opposed to a detective).
To clothe in a uniform.
Unvarying; all the same.
Consistent; conforming to one standard.
With speed of convergence not depending on choice of function argument; as in uniform continuity, uniform convergence
(of a polymer) Composed of a single macromolecular species.
(of a polyhedron) That is isogonal and whose faces are regular polygons; (of an n-dimensional (n>3) polytope) that is isogonal and whose bounding (n-1)-dimensional facets are uniform polytopes.
Any bird of the genus Falco, all of which are birds of prey.
A female such bird, a male being a tiercel.
A light cannon used from the 15th to the 17th century; a falconet.
To hunt with a falcon or falcons.
A teleporter.
A satellite ground station.
To travel, often instantaneously, from one point to another without physically crossing the distance between the two points.
To move (an object) in this fashion, as by telekinesis.
View, natural features, landscape.
Stage backdrops, property and other items on a stage that give the impression of the location of the scene.
Jolly and full of high spirits.
definitions.example: We had a very merry Christmas.

definitions.definition: Festive and full of fun and laughter.
definitions.example: Everyone was merry at the party.

definitions.definition: Brisk
definitions.example: The play moved along at a merry pace.

definitions.definition: Causing laughter, mirth, gladness, or delight.
definitions.example: a merry jest

definitions.definition: Drunk; tipsy
definitions.example: Some of us got a little merry at the office Christmas party.

definitions.definition: A young sexually attractive woman.
Of an age suitable for marriage; marriageable (principally of a young woman).
Sexually attractive (especially of a young woman).
Easily discovered, seen, or understood; self-explanatory.
The second of the two terms by which a point is referred to, in a system of fixed rectilinear coordinate (Cartesian coordinate) axes.
The vertical line representing an axis of a Cartesian coordinate system, on which the ordinate (sense above) is shown.
To ordain a priest, or consecrate a bishop
To align a series of objects
Arranged regularly in rows; orderly; disposed or arranged in an orderly or regular fashion.
A risky or daring undertaking or journey.
An event that is not, or cannot be, foreseen.
The thing risked; especially, something sent to sea in trade.
To undertake a risky or daring journey.
To risk or offer.
definitions.example: Nothing ventured, nothing gained.

definitions.definition: To dare to engage in; to attempt without any certainty of success. Used with at or on
To put or send on a venture or chance.
definitions.example: to venture a horse to the West Indies

definitions.definition: To confide in; to rely on; to trust.
To say something.
An educational institution for young children, usually between ages 4 and 6; nursery school.
The elementary school grade before first grade.
The two levels between nursery and prep; the second and third years of preschool.
The trachea.
A flight path used by aeroplanes.
A crazed Norse warrior who fought in a frenzy; a berserker.
Injuriously, maniacally, or furiously violent or out of control.
definitions.example: After seeing his sister stabbed to death, he went berserk and attacked the killer like a wild animal.

definitions.definition: Weird; bizarre.
The work involved in performing an activity; exertion.
definitions.example: It took a lot of effort to find a decent-sized, fully-furnished apartment within walking distance of the office.   He made a conscious effort to not appear affected by the stories in the paper.

definitions.definition: An endeavour.
definitions.example: Although he didn't win any medals, Johnson's effort at the Olympics won over many fans.

definitions.definition: A force acting on a body in the direction of its motion.
To make an effort.
To strengthen, fortify or stimulate
Inspiring or deserving love or affection.
A proposal that has been made.
definitions.example: I decline your offer to contract.

definitions.definition: Something put forth, bid, proffered or tendered.
definitions.example: His offer was $3.50 per share.

definitions.definition: An invitation to enter into a binding contract communicated to another party which contains terms sufficiently definite to create an enforceable contract if the other party accepts the invitation.
definitions.example: His first letter was not a real offer, but an attempt to determine interest.

definitions.definition: To propose or express one's willingness (to do something).
definitions.example: She offered to help with her homework.

definitions.definition: To present in words; to proffer; to make a proposal of; to suggest.
definitions.example: Everybody offered an opinion.

definitions.definition: To place at someone’s disposal; to present (something) to be either accepted or turned down.
definitions.example: He offered use of his car for the week.  He offered his good will for the Councilman's vote.

definitions.definition: To present (something) to God or gods as a gesture of worship, or for a sacrifice.
To place (something) in a position where it can be added to an existing mechanical assembly.
To bid, as a price, reward, or wages.
definitions.example: I offered twenty dollars for it. The company is offering a salary of £30,000 a year.

definitions.definition: To happen, to present itself.
To make an attempt; typically used with at.
To put in opposition to; to manifest in an offensive way; to threaten.
definitions.example: to offer violence to somebody

definitions.definition: (used in combinations from phrasal verbs) agent noun of off
The result of multiplying a given number of consecutive integers from 1 to the given number. In equations, it is symbolized by an exclamation mark (!). For example, 5! = 1 × 2 × 3 × 4 × 5 = 120.
Of or pertaining to a factor or factorial.
Of or pertaining to a factor, a kind of business agent.
Of or pertaining to a factory.
An increase in size, number, value, or strength.
definitions.example: Growth was dampened by a softening of the global economy in 2001, but picked up in the subsequent years due to strong growth in China.

definitions.definition: The act of growing, getting bigger or higher.
Something that grows or has grown.
An abnormal mass such as a tumor.
Any word used as the key to a code.
Any word used in a reference work to link to other words or other information.
A reserved word used to identify a specific command, function etc.
Any word that occurs in a text more often than normal.
To tag with keywords, as for example to facilitate searching.
A person who is anti-fun, or prevents others from having fun.
The action of squeezing a small amount of a person's skin and flesh, making it hurt.
A close compression of anything with the fingers.
definitions.example: I gave the leather of the sofa a pinch, gauging the texture.

definitions.definition: A small amount of powder or granules, such that the amount could be held between fingertip and thumb tip.
An awkward situation of some kind (especially money or social) which is difficult to escape.
A metal bar used as a lever for lifting weights, rolling wheels, etc.
An organic herbal smoke additive.
A magnetic compression of an electrically-conducting filament.
The narrow part connecting the two bulbs of an hourglass.
An arrest.
To squeeze a small amount of a person's skin and flesh, making it hurt.
definitions.example: The children were scolded for pinching each other.

definitions.definition: To squeeze between the thumb and forefinger.
To squeeze between two objects.
To steal, usually something inconsequential.
definitions.example: Someone has pinched my handkerchief!

definitions.definition: To arrest or capture.
To cut shoots or buds of a plant in order to shape the plant, or to improve its yield.
To sail so close-hauled that the sails begin to flutter.
To take hold; to grip, as a dog does.
To be stingy or covetous; to live sparingly.
To seize; to grip; to bite; said of animals.
To cramp; to straiten; to oppress; to starve.
definitions.example: to be pinched for money

definitions.definition: To move, as a railroad car, by prying the wheels with a pinch.
To complain or find fault.
In a contrary direction to.
definitions.example: It is hard work to swim against the current.

definitions.definition: In physical opposition to; in collision with.
definitions.example: The rain pounds against the window.

definitions.definition: In physical contact with, so as to abut or be supported by.
definitions.example: The kennel was put against the back wall.

definitions.definition: Close to, alongside.
definitions.example: A row of trees stood against a fence.

definitions.definition: In front of; before (a background).
definitions.example: The giant was silhouetted against the door.

definitions.definition: In contrast or comparison with.
definitions.example: He stands out against his classmates.

definitions.definition: In competition with, versus.
definitions.example: The Tigers will play against the Bears this weekend.

definitions.definition: Contrary to; in conflict with.
definitions.example: Doing this is against my principles.

definitions.definition: In opposition to.
definitions.example: Are you against freedom of choice?

definitions.definition: Of betting odds, denoting a worse-than-even chance.
definitions.example: That horse is fifty-to-one against, so it has virtually no chance of winning.

definitions.definition: In exchange for.
definitions.example: The vouchers are redeemable against West End shows and theatre breaks.

definitions.definition: As counterbalance to.
As a charge on.
definitions.example: Tax is levied against income from sales.

definitions.definition: As protection from.
definitions.example: He turned the umbrella against the wind.

definitions.definition: In anticipation of; in preparation for (a particular time, event etc.).
definitions.example: The stores are kept well stocked against a time of need.

definitions.definition: (Hollywood) To be paid now in contrast to the following amount to be paid later under specified circumstances, usually that a movie is made or has started filming.
definitions.example: The studio weren't sure the movie would ever get made, so they only paid $50,000 against $200,000. That way they wouldn't be out very much if filming never began.

definitions.definition: Exposed to.
By the time that (something happened); before.
Resembling the metal copper, especially in color.
Curved or swept upwards
With respect to place; in place.
definitions.example: These things are locally separated.

definitions.definition: In or from the local area.
definitions.example: In this shop we only sell products farmed locally.

definitions.definition: In a restricted part of the body.
definitions.example: To be applied locally

definitions.definition: Within a sufficiently small sphere (or circle or interval) around a given point (sometimes, around any point).
definitions.example: locally Euclidean

definitions.definition: A historically constituted, stable community of people, formed on the basis of a common language, territory, economic life, ethnicity and/or psychological make-up manifested in a common culture.
definitions.example: The Kurdish people constitute a nation in the Middle East

definitions.definition: A sovereign state.
definitions.example: Though legally single nations, many states comprise several distinct cultural or ethnic groups.

definitions.definition: An association of students based on its members' birthplace or ethnicity.
definitions.example: Once widespread across Europe in medieval times, nations are now largely restricted to the ancient universities of Sweden and Finland.

definitions.definition: A great number; a great deal.
Any of several marine molluscs of the family Octopodidae, having no internal or external protective shell or bone (unlike the nautilus, squid and cuttlefish) and eight arms each covered with suckers.
The flesh of these marine molluscs eaten as food.
An organization that has many powerful branches controlled from the centre.
To put (or attempt to put) one's fingers, hands or arms in many things or places at roughly the same time.
To spread out in long arms or legs in many directions.
To plug a large number of devices into a single electric outlet.
(by extension) To grow in use vastly beyond what was originally intended.
To hunt and catch octopuses.
To behave like an octopus.
In an optimal manner.
A type of small fish, Carassius auratus, typically orange-colored.
definitions.example: For the children's fair we'll have a few games they can play, so that each child "wins" a goldfish as a prize.

definitions.definition: A belvedere, either a type of summer-house or a roofed, detached porch-like structure, usually in a yard, park or lawn.
A small African monkey, Cercopithecus aethiops or Chlorocebus pygerythrus, having a long tail, a black face with white cheek tufts and a greenish-brown coat
A blow that produces a muffled sound.
The sound of such a blow; a thud.
Used to replace the vulgar or blasphemous element in "what the hell" and similar phrases.
definitions.example: Where the thump have you been?!

definitions.definition: To hit (someone or something) as if to make a thump.
To cause to make a thumping sound.
definitions.example: The cat thumped its tail in irritation.

definitions.definition: To thud or pound.
To throb with a muffled rhythmic sound.
definitions.example: Dance music thumped from the nightclub entrance.

definitions.definition: To mine using the technique of hydraulic mining.
Pertaining to water.
Related to, or operated by, hydraulics.
definitions.example: A hydraulic press is operated by the differential pressure of water on pistons of different dimensions.

definitions.definition: A prize or award.
Something offered at a reduced price as an inducement to buy something else.
A bonus paid in addition to normal payments.
The amount to be paid for an insurance policy.
An unusually high value.
The amount by which a security's value exceeds its face value.
Superior in quality; higher in price or value.
High-end; belonging to the market segment between mid-market and luxury.
To agree, verify or concur; to answer positively.
definitions.example: She affirmed that she would go when I asked her.

definitions.definition: To assert positively; to tell with confidence; to aver; to maintain as true.
To support or encourage.
definitions.example: They did everything they could to affirm the children's self-confidence.

definitions.definition: To make firm; to confirm, or ratify; especially to assert or confirm, as a judgment, decree, or order, brought before an appellate court for review.
Any of a class of organic compounds (such as ethanol) containing a hydroxyl functional group (-OH).
Ethanol.
Beverages containing ethanol, collectively.
Any very fine powder.
Any private area, (particularly) bowers in the open air.
Any private or inner room, (particularly):
A pew or side-chapel reserved for a monarch or other feudal lord.
A private cabinet, (particularly):
Any small room or side-room, (particularly):
An ordinary similar to a bar but half as broad.
A sewer.
To shut away for private discussion.
definitions.example: The ambassador has been closeted with the prime minister all afternoon. We're all worried what will be announced when they exit.

definitions.definition: To put into a private place for a secret interview or interrogation.
To shut up in, or as in, a closet for concealment or confinement.
Private.
Secret, (especially) with reference to gay people who are in the closet; closeted.
To reverse the process of scrambling, decrypt.
To put into order or restore to order.
(usually childish) Mother.
To treat someone like a mother would; to mother someone.
(chiefly in the superlative) Characteristic of a mother; motherly.
definitions.example: She is the mommiest of mommies.

definitions.definition: A state or situation of great confusion, disorder, trouble or destruction; chaos.
definitions.example: In all the mayhem, some children were separated from their partners.

definitions.definition: Infliction of violent injury on a person or thing.
definitions.example: The fighting dogs created mayhem in the flower beds.

definitions.definition: The maiming of a person by depriving him of the use of any of his limbs which are necessary for defense or protection.
The crime of damaging things or harming people on purpose.
In the form of a cloud or haze; hazy.
Vague or ill-defined.
Relating to a nebula or nebulae.
(passive, constructed with "with") To be preoccupied with a single topic or emotion.
definitions.example: Some people are obsessed with sports.

definitions.definition: To dominate the thoughts of someone.
definitions.example: Thoughts of her obsess my every waking moment.

definitions.definition: (construed with over) To think or talk obsessively about.
definitions.example: Stop obsessing over it, will you!

definitions.definition: A viscous preparation of oils and/or fats, usually containing medication, used as a treatment or as an emollient.
A substance used to anoint, as in religious rituals.
A design involving flowers
A perfume redolent of flowers
Of, pertaining to, or connected with flowers.
Portraying flowers, especially in a stylized way.
definitions.example: I really like the floral pattern on this Chinese vase.

definitions.definition: A mythological undead creature said to feed on the blood of the living.
A person with the medical condition systemic lupus erythematosus, colloquially known as vampirism, with effects such as photosensitivity and brownish-red stained teeth.
A blood-sucking bat; vampire bat (Desmodus rotundus)
A person who drains one's time, energy, money, etc.
A vamp: a seductive woman who exploits men.
A medical technician who works with patients' blood.
To drain of energy or resources.
The ability to be enforced, or to be enforced to any useful effect.

definitions.definition: To grow teeth.

definitions.definition: To bite on something to relieve discomfort caused by growing teeth.
definitions.example: She'll teethe on anything that she can get into her mouth.

definitions.definition: A hard, calcareous structure present in the mouth of many vertebrate animals, generally used for eating.
A sharp projection on the blade of a saw or similar implement.
A projection on the edge of a gear that meshes with similar projections on adjacent gears, or on the circumference of a cog that engages with a chain.
A projection or point in other parts of the body resembling the tooth of a vertebrate animal.
A pointed projection from the margin of a leaf.
The rough surface of some kinds of cel or other films that allows better adhesion of artwork.
Liking, fondness (compare toothsome).

definitions.definition: An irreducible component of a comb that intersects the handle in exactly one point, that point being distinct from the unique point of intersection for any other tooth of the comb.
An expression in which the eyes are partly closed.
The look of eyes which are turned in different directions, as in strabismus.

definitions.definition: A quick or sideways glance.
A short look.
A hagioscope.
(radio transmission) The angle by which the transmission signal is offset from the normal of a phased array antenna.
To look with the eyes partly closed, as in bright sunlight, or as a threatening expression.

definitions.definition: To look or glance sideways.
To look with, or have eyes that are turned in different directions; to suffer from strabismus.
To have an indirect bearing, reference, or implication; to have an allusion to, or inclination towards, something.
To be not quite straight, off-centred; to deviate from a true line; to run obliquely.
To turn to an oblique position; to direct obliquely.

definitions.definition: Looking obliquely; having the vision distorted.
Askew, not level
A bitless headpiece of rope or straps, placed on the head of animals such as cattle or horses to lead or tie them.
A rope with a noose, for hanging criminals; the gallows rope.
A halter top.
To place a halter on.

definitions.definition: An amount of money paid out.
The value of dividends paid to shareholders.
Pleasantness.

definitions.definition: A thing or circumstance that is welcome and makes life a little easier or more pleasant.

definitions.definition: Convenience.
A unit pertaining to the infrastructure of a community, such as a public toilet, a postbox, a library etc.
A keen-edged knife of peculiar shape, used in shaving the hair from the face or other parts of the body.
Any tool or instrument designed for shaving.
The sharp tusk of a wild boar.
A conceptual device that allows one to shave away unlikely explanations for a phenomenon.

definitions.definition: A card game with many rule variants, conceptually similar to mahjong.
A written attack or other work ridiculing a person, group, or institution.
To satirize or poke fun at.
A homogeneous mass of rock, not layered and without an obvious crystal structure.

definitions.definition: A group of people from a locality, or sharing a collective aim, interest, etc.

definitions.definition: Very large in size or extent

definitions.definition: Very large or bulky and heavy and solid

definitions.definition: To a very great extent; total, utter.
Of particularly exceptional quality or value; awesome.

definitions.definition: Outstanding, beautiful

definitions.definition: Affecting a large portion of the body, or severe.

definitions.definition: Having a large mass
(of a particle) Possessing mass.

definitions.definition: Homogenous, unstructured

An army of trained civilians, which may be an official reserve army, called upon in time of need, the entire able-bodied population of a state which may also be called upon, or a private force not under government control.
The police in the Soviet Union and some related or successor states (e.g. modern Belarus).
A type of paint with a solvent that evaporates to leave a hard, transparent, glossy film.
Anything resembling such a paint; glossy appearance.

A passenger train, probably derived from the varnished passenger cars used at one time.
To apply varnish.
To cover up with varnish.
To gloss over a defect.
A risky or daring undertaking or journey.
An event that is not, or cannot be, foreseen.
The thing risked; especially, something sent to sea in trade.
The derivation of sexual satisfaction by watching people secretly, especially when those being watched are undressed or engaging in sexual activity.

An actual, real one; notably:
Relating to a person's acts or deeds; active, practical
Existing in reality, not just potentially; really acted or acting; occurring in fact.

definitions.definition: In action at the time being; now existing; current.
Used as intensifier to emphasise a following noun; exact, very.

definitions.definition: (by extension) discarded odds and ends

definitions.definition: Of, relating to, or resembling rubber, especially in consistency.

A person who rules or governs; someone or something that exercises dominion or controlling power over others.

An upward current of air, especially a strong one

definitions.definition: The innate characteristics of a thing. What something will tend by its own constitution, to be or do. Distinct from what might be expected or intended.
The summary of everything that has to do with biological, chemical and physical states and events in the physical universe.
Conformity to that which is natural, as distinguished from that which is artificial, or forced, or remote from actual experience.
Kind, sort; character; quality.
Physical constitution or existence; the vital powers; the natural life.

Having slanted or oblique lines or markings.
Having a slanted or oblique direction.
Of or related to the cater-corner (diagonally opposite) legs of a quadruped, whether the front left and back right or front right and back left.

To clothe.
Any of the joints between the phalanges of the fingers.

definitions.definition: The rounded point where a flat changes to a slope on a piste.
To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
To obtain the services of in return for fixed payment.

definitions.definition: To employ; to obtain the services of (a person) in exchange for remuneration; to give someone a job.

definitions.definition: To accomplish by paying for services.

definitions.definition: To accept employment.
definitions.example: They hired out as day laborers.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.

definitions.definition: The act of telling or engaging in jokes.
A manufactured regenerated cellulosic fiber.
An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.

definitions.definition: That part of a hall of legislation not appropriated to the official use of the assembly.

An apartment or passageway in the fore part of an old-fashioned cabin under the quarter-deck.
A confined place for cattle, formed by hedges, trees, or other fencing, near the farmyard.
A margin along either side of the playing field in the sport of kabaddi.
To attempt to influence (a public official or decision-maker) in favor of a specific opinion or cause.

definitions.definition: A disappointment or anticlimax.

Material applied to a wound for protection or therapy.
A sauce, especially a cold one for salads.
Something added to the soil as a fertilizer etc.
The activity of getting dressed.
Dress; raiment; especially, ornamental habiliment or attire.
The stuffing of fowls, pigs, etc.; forcemeat.

Castigation; scolding; dressing down.
The process of extracting metals or other valuable components from minerals
Something which is free; a giveaway or handout.

definitions.definition: The act of discharging a firearm.
The sound made by a discharging firearm.
The distance to which shot can be thrown from a gun; the reach or range of a gun.
A bullet, projectile, or other shot fired from a gun.
A reduction in speed, or a decrease in the level of production, etc.
Friend
(chiefly California) Mexican
A native of the Philippines who was friendly toward the Spanish.
An amusing story.
Something said or done for amusement, not in seriousness.

definitions.definition: The root cause or main issue, especially an unexpected one
A laughably worthless thing or person; a sham.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.

definitions.definition: Really good.
Funny or entertaining.

To separate (something that is wound up)

definitions.definition: To disentangle
To relax; to chill out; to rest and relieve of stress

definitions.definition: To be or become unwound; to be capable of being unwound or untwisted.
To undo something.
To analyse (a call stack) so as to generate a stack trace etc.
Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually involving the engagement of military forces.

definitions.definition: Instruments of war.
Armed forces.
A particular card game for two players, notable for having its outcome predetermined by how the cards are dealt.

To carry on, as a contest; to wage.
A guard or watchman.
A chief administrative officer of a prison.

To carry out the duties of a warden.
A computerized interactive system for storing, processing and reproducing verbal messages left through a conventional telephone network.

Something that is variable.

A symbol representing a variable.

Marked by diversity or difference.
Having no fixed quantitative value.
Tending to deviate from a normal or recognized type.

The giving birth of kittens
A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.

A brave, chivalrous and honorable man devoted to a noble cause or love interest.
